questions.question: Which tag is used to define the root of an HTML document?
questions.explanation: The <html> tag is the root element that wraps all content in an HTML document, including the <head> and <body> sections.

questions.question: What is the correct order of elements in a basic HTML document?
questions.explanation: A valid HTML document starts with <!DOCTYPE html>, followed by <html>, then <head> for metadata, and <body> for visible content.

questions.question: Which section of an HTML document contains metadata such as the title and character encoding?
questions.explanation: The <head> section contains metadata about the document, including the <title>, <meta> charset declaration, linked stylesheets, and scripts.

questions.question: What does the <title> tag define?
choices: A heading displayed on the page
The title shown in the browser tab
questions.explanation: The <title> tag sets the text displayed in the browser's title bar or tab. It is placed inside the <head> section and is not visible on the page itself.

questions.question: The ___ tag contains all the visible content that is displayed in the browser window.
questions.explanation: The <body> tag wraps all content that is rendered and visible to the user in the browser, such as text, images, links, and other elements.

questions.question: To specify the character encoding as UTF-8, you add a ___ tag inside the <head> section.
questions.explanation: The <meta charset="UTF-8"> tag declares the character encoding for the document, ensuring special characters are displayed correctly.
